name: Starbucks Yelp Review Starter Codebook
intent: A starter taxonomy for thematic analysis of Starbucks customer reviews. Students extend it as part of the exercise; this is deliberately not the 'final' codebook.
instructions_for_coder: Apply a theme only when the review contains a specific sentence or phrase that supports it. Be conservative: a review can map to zero, one, or several themes. For each applied theme, capture the verbatim quote span (max ~25 words). Do not invent themes outside this list.

themes.name: Mobile App & Mobile Order
themes.definition: Experience using the Starbucks app to order ahead, pay, or earn rewards. Includes bugs, ordering flow, in-store pickup handoff, and app-driven loyalty interactions.
examples: ordered on the app and it wasn't ready when I got there
the app crashed at checkout
love that I can order ahead and just walk in
themes.business_question: Where does our digital ordering experience break down, and how does it affect store-level satisfaction?

themes.name: Speed & Wait Time
themes.definition: Customer perception of how fast they were served, including line length, time-to-drink, and any delays. Does NOT include drive-thru-specific speed (see drive_thru).
examples: took 20 minutes for a drip coffee
line was out the door but moved fast
way too slow during the morning rush
themes.business_question: Where are throughput bottlenecks hurting the customer experience, and at what time of day?

themes.name: Drink Quality & Customization
themes.definition: How the beverage was made: taste, temperature, consistency with what was ordered, handling of customizations, and recipe execution. Includes complaints like 'made wrong' and praise for getting it right.
examples: my latte tasted burnt
they nailed my half-caf oat milk request
the cold brew was watered down
themes.business_question: Are baristas executing the menu consistently, and which drinks have the most quality variance?

themes.name: Food Quality
themes.definition: Quality of pastries, sandwiches, and other food items. Includes freshness, temperature, portion size, and whether food matches what's pictured or described.
examples: the breakfast sandwich was ice cold in the middle
the banana bread is always fresh
no croissants left at 9am
themes.business_question: Is our food program holding up its end of the ticket, and which items underdeliver?

themes.name: Barista Service & Friendliness
themes.definition: Interpersonal experience with staff: warmth, attentiveness, rudeness, personal recognition (name on cup, regulars), recommendations, and recovery when something goes wrong.
examples: the barista remembered my name and my usual
completely ignored when I walked in
they were so patient with my picky order
themes.business_question: Where is the partner experience translating (or failing to translate) into the customer experience?

themes.name: Cleanliness & Maintenance
themes.definition: State of the store: tables, floors, bathrooms, condiment bar, exterior, and visible upkeep. Includes maintenance issues like broken equipment or dirty signage.
examples: tables were covered in old cups
bathroom was disgusting
always spotless when I come in
themes.business_question: Which locations are slipping on operational hygiene standards, and is it correlated with rating drops?

themes.name: Atmosphere & Workspace
themes.definition: The 'third place' experience: ambiance, music, seating, lighting, comfort for hanging out or working (wifi, outlets, noise level, table availability). Excludes cleanliness (see cleanliness).
examples: great place to get work done, lots of outlets
music was way too loud
no seats anywhere at 2pm on a Saturday
themes.business_question: Is the 'third place' promise still landing, and where are we losing café-as-workspace customers?

themes.name: Pricing & Value
themes.definition: Customer perception of cost relative to what they got. Includes outright price complaints, comparisons to competitors, and 'worth it' / 'not worth it' framings.
examples: $7 for a small latte is highway robbery
worth every penny
Dunkin gives you twice as much for half the price
themes.business_question: Where are we crossing customer price-sensitivity thresholds, and is the value perception eroding?

themes.name: Rewards & Loyalty Program
themes.definition: Anything related to Starbucks Rewards, Stars, free drinks, BOGO promotions, or membership perks. Includes confusion or frustration with the program mechanics.
examples: used my birthday reward and the barista didn't know how to ring it up
love saving up Stars for free drinks
they changed the rewards program and now it's worthless
themes.business_question: Is the rewards program driving loyalty and what's friction in redemption?

themes.name: Drive-Thru Experience
themes.definition: Drive-thru-specific experience: speaker clarity, line length, window handoff, order accuracy through the drive-thru channel. Mark only when the customer is clearly using the drive-thru.
examples: drive thru line wrapped around the building
they handed me the wrong drink at the window
fastest drive thru in town
themes.business_question: Is the drive-thru channel meeting its speed-of-service targets, and where is order accuracy slipping?

extension_prompt: Students: this codebook is intentionally incomplete. Read 20-30 reviews from your assigned city, find at least one theme that's present in the data but missing from this list, and propose it. Good candidates often emerge around: parking & accessibility, hours of operation, specific menu launches, holiday cups & seasonal items, kid-friendliness, or competitor comparisons.
